leagueBriefing: Ryan Blaney overcame early pit road penalties to secure a dramatic win at Phoenix, completing a perfect weekend for Team Penske following their IndyCar victory on Saturday.

games.headline: Blaney rallies from early penalties to complete Penske's Phoenix sweep
games.brief: Ryan Blaney showcased pure perseverance at Phoenix Raceway, storming back from two separate trips to the rear of the field to claim his first win of 2026. A strategic two-tire call on the final pit stop allowed Blaney to jump ahead of a dominant Christopher Bell, who led a race-high 176 laps but couldn't close the gap on fresh rubber. The victory capped off a historic weekend for Team Penske, following Josef Newgarden's IndyCar win at the same track just 24 hours prior.
bullets: Ryan Blaney overcame a 'pitting outside the box' penalty to secure his 18th career victory and second at Phoenix Raceway.
Christopher Bell dominated the afternoon with 176 laps led and a Stage 2 win, but fell just 0.399 seconds short at the finish line.
The race featured 12 cautions, tying the all-time track record for yellow flags at the Arizona one-mile oval.
Tyler Reddick's historic three-race winning streak to start the season ended with an eighth-place finish, though he maintains the points lead.
Ty Gibbs earned his second consecutive fourth-place finish, leading late before being overtaken by Blaney with 10 laps to go.
games.context: The win vaults Blaney to second in the championship standings as the series heads to Las Vegas.
